top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.assessment_focus: EMERGENCY TRIGGER — MATERIAL DEVELOPMENT. On May 8 President Trump announced a three-day ceasefire (May 9–11) covering suspension of all kinetic activity and a 1,000-prisoner swap. Both Ukraine (Zelenskyy confirmed on X) and Russia (Ushakov confirmed for Kremlin) accepted. This is structurally different from the collapsed unilateral ceasefires of May 5–7: it is a trilateral US-brokered agreement with explicit presidential confirmation from both parties. The May 7 assessment applied the bad-faith-ceasefire rule and confirmed 50.0 Functional. The question for the assessor is whether this new ceasefire constitutes evidence of ACT or INT movement — or whether the bad-faith rule applies again pending verification of May 9–11 conduct. Primary dimension focus: INT (commitment credibility — does a US-brokered agreed ceasefire alter the counterparty-record analysis?), ACT (prisoner swap as concrete humanitarian action). If ceasefire holds through May 9–11, this is a material positive signal. If it collapses again, the Russia counterparty record is further reinforced. RECOMMEND: assess on May 9 scan after observing first 24h of ceasefire conduct. Flag for May 9 re-queue regardless of current assessment outcome.
top_entities.news_summary: Trump announced three-day ceasefire May 9–11 on May 8. Both Zelenskyy and Kremlin aide Ushakov confirmed. Includes 1,000-prisoner swap. First agreed trilateral ceasefire after collapse of all prior unilateral attempts. Outcome of May 9–11 window is the scoring-relevant evidence. This is the Victory Day re-queue trigger from the May 7 mandatory carry-over.
top_entities.assessor_note: Route to May 9 scan for post-ceasefire evidence collection. May 8 announcement is the trigger; May 9–11 conduct is the scoring evidence.

top_entities.name: India
top_entities.assessment_focus: MAJOR NEW CONTEXT — ONE-YEAR ANNIVERSARY OF INDIA-PAKISTAN CONFLICT (May 7-8, 2026). The one-year anniversary of Operation Sindoor (launched May 7, 2025) produced significant new compassion-relevant evidence in this window. (1) India has yet to reinstate the Indus Waters Treaty, suspended April 23, 2025 — the treaty supplies 80%+ of Pakistan's agricultural water and sustains 240M+ livelihoods. Its continued suspension is a structural humanitarian constraint. (2) India centralized war command post-conflict, with Kashmir Media Service reporting military reorganization. (3) HRW World Report 2026 documents hat speech surge against Muslims following the conflict (64+ incidents in first 10 days), media account blocking by government (ordered X to block 8,000+ accounts without evidence or justification), and online censorship. (4) India's NDTV carried coverage framing the conflict as military success without civilian harm acknowledgment. Last assessed May 6 — reassess is warranted given the anniversary context producing new consolidated documentary evidence of IWT harm and domestic rights rollback.
top_entities.news_summary: One-year anniversary of India-Pakistan conflict on May 7-8 triggered new coverage documenting: (1) Indus Waters Treaty still suspended (sustains 240M Pakistani livelihoods), (2) government-ordered blocking of 8,000+ X accounts during conflict, (3) Muslim hate speech surge, (4) war command centralization. India vows to crush 'terror ecosystem.' Multiple compassion-dimension impacts.

top_entities.name: Pakistan
top_entities.assessment_focus: BOUNDARY WATCH ACTIVE + ANNIVERSARY TRIGGER. Pakistan composite reported as 20.3 in May 7 digest (0.3 above Critical/Developing band boundary at 20) but rotation-state shows 15.3 — discrepancy requires assessor reconciliation. Anniversary of India-Pakistan conflict (May 7-8) produced the following scoring-relevant evidence in window: (1) Pakistan military issued formal strong-response warning vowing 'greater strength, precision and resolve' against future aggression — ACC and INT signals. (2) Pakistan marks conflict while diplomatic relations and trust remain frozen — no restoration of Indus Waters Treaty talks. (3) Genocide Watch published Pakistan Country Report May 2026 — requires review for any new structural designations. (4) Ceasefire holds (one year) but military buildup and naval exercises in Arabian Sea suggest continued posture escalation. IWT suspension directly harms Pakistan's 240M+ agricultural population — EMP impact. Key assessment question: does the anniversary posture escalation and IWT continued suspension justify downward movement from current composite?
top_entities.news_summary: One-year anniversary of 2025 conflict. Pakistan military issued strong-response warning. Diplomatic relations frozen. IWT suspension continues (80%+ of agricultural water supply affected). Genocide Watch Country Report May 2026 published. Ceasefire technically holds but military posture escalating. Boundary watch: composite 20.3 per May 7 digest (0.3 above Critical floor at 20).

top_entities.name: Meta
top_entities.assessment_focus: PRIORITY FIRST BASELINE — active child safety trial in window. Phase 2 bench trial opened May 4, 2026 in New Mexico. Phase 1 jury found Meta liable for 75,000 violations of New Mexico Unfair Practices Act (March 2026) and ordered $375M in civil penalties. Phase 2 (ongoing through May 8 window) seeks court-ordered reforms: age verification, safer recommendation algorithms, restrictions on end-to-end encryption for minors, court-supervised child safety monitor. New Mexico AG warned Meta's behavior is 'showing the world how little it cares about child safety.' Meta threatened to remove platforms from New Mexico if demands met. The judge opened May 4 warning he won't 'overreach.' This is the highest-profile active consumer-product-safety trial involving a Fortune 500 company in the pipeline. Cross-reference Character.AI floor designation: Meta's scale (billions of users) and child harm documentation is extensive but different in kind (user-generated harm facilitation vs. product-design direct harm). Assess: EMP (child safety architecture), BND (monetization practices facilitating minor harm), ACC (transparency of safety-incident reporting), ACT (response trajectory — phase 2 trial engagement vs. threats to exit state).
top_entities.news_summary: Phase 2 bench trial opened May 4 in New Mexico seeking court-ordered reforms to child safety architecture. Phase 1 $375M verdict already issued. Meta threatened to leave New Mexico rather than comply. AG characterized Meta's position as demonstrating indifference to child safety. Active trial in window makes this time-sensitive for first baseline.

top_entities.name: Myanmar
top_entities.assessment_focus: FLOOR ENTITY STRUCTURAL SCAN — new airstrike pattern confirmed in window. Junta airstrikes are daily in the May window: Sagaing hospital attack May 7 (two PDF members receiving medical treatment killed — hospital targeting); Chin State Tedim Township May 6 (nine killed including five women in PDF base); Chin State Kanpetlet May 2 (nine civilians killed at bridge repair including children). Hospital targeting (May 7) is a potential structural-change signal — direct targeting of medical facilities is distinct from combat-zone incidental harm and may warrant structural-change documentation if part of a systematic pattern. Fortify Rights November 2025 report documented destruction of schools, churches, medical facilities. Assess: is the May 7 hospital attack a continuation of documented pattern (no structural change from floor) or an escalation into systematic medical-facility targeting (new floor conduct documentation)? Recommend assessor review the Sagaing hospital attack and Fortify Rights documentation before determining if a conduct documentation is warranted.
top_entities.news_summary: Daily airstrikes confirmed: hospital attack May 7 (two killed), nine killed May 6 in Tedim, nine civilians killed May 2 at bridge repair. Hospital targeting may require structural-change conduct documentation. Floor entity — assess only for structural-change signals.
top_entities.assessor_note: Assess only for structural-change conduct documentation — not a full re-score. Focus: is hospital targeting on May 7 a new systematic pattern or continuation of documented floor conduct?

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.assessment_focus: FLOOR ENTITY STRUCTURAL SCAN — OCHA Situation Report May 1, 2026 confirms ongoing airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire resulting in civilian casualties. 72,599 Palestinians killed as of April 29, 2026. OCHA six-month ceasefire review notes ceasefire has 'saved lives' but risks remain. Funding crisis: only 10% of 2026 humanitarian operations funded. UNRWA Situation Reports 218–220 document conditions. The May 8 evidence window shows: displacement ongoing, health/sanitation crisis (scabies, chickenpox, rodent infestation), strike activity continuing. This is continuation of existing floor pattern — no structural change warranting new documentation in this specific window. Do not assess.
top_entities.news_summary: OCHA May 1 report confirms ongoing airstrikes and civilian casualties. 72,599 killed through April 29. Humanitarian funding at 10%. Conditions constitute continuous floor conduct — no structural change in May 8 window.
top_entities.assessor_note: Do not assess. Continuous floor conduct. Next scheduled re-queue TBD — monitor for structural change only.

top_entities.name: Aleph Alpha
top_entities.assessment_focus: FIRST BASELINE — highest-ranked unassessed ai-labs entity. Aleph Alpha (German, sovereign AI focus) has composite 76.4 Established band (published). No agent baseline exists. Volatility: composite 76.4 is near the Established/Exemplary boundary at 80 — 3.6 points below band change, which triggers 10-pt volatility score. First baseline should test whether the published 76.4 holds. Sector context: EU AI Act GPAI enforcement (August 2, 2026) applies to Aleph Alpha as a European provider — pre-enforcement baseline is important for August queue. The Pentagon AI cohort pressure on European AI labs (Aleph Alpha positioned as NATO-aligned sovereign AI alternative) provides structural context. Assess: ACC (German sovereign AI transparency commitments), SYS (government partnerships and their terms), BND (defense-adjacent applications and restrictions).
top_entities.news_summary: Highest-ranked unassessed ai-labs entity. Composite 76.4 near Established/Exemplary boundary. German sovereign AI positioning. EU AI Act GPAI enforcement August 2 makes pre-enforcement baseline important. No prior agent assessment.

top_entities.name: Monaco
top_entities.assessment_focus: ROTATION BACKFILL — First baseline. Monaco rank 45 (countries), composite 51.6 Functional. Micro-state with extremely high per-capita wealth and no income tax. Governance: constitutional monarchy. Known constraints: freedom of expression and press limited by lèse-majesté laws, no domestic asylum process (transfers asylum seekers to France). Assess: EQU (wealth concentration in micro-state context), ACC (institutional transparency of princely government), BND (scope of protection — stateless residents and migrant workers who constitute majority of population).
top_entities.news_summary: Never assessed. Rotation backfill candidate. Composite 51.6 Functional. Micro-state context — large non-citizen population (majority), wealth concentration, lèse-majesté press restrictions.
